source_note: Auto-refreshed 2026-06-27 via LiteLLM pricing database. GPU providers without public pricing APIs kept from previous snapshot — verify manually at: https://www.coreweave.com/gpu-cloud-computing, https://aws.amazon.com/machine-learning/trainium/pricing/, https://cloud.google.com/tpu/pricing

llama_31_8b.comparable_api: Claude Haiku / GPT-4o Mini quality range
llama_31_8b.notes: Meta open-weight. Excellent throughput on any GPU. Best for simple classification, FAQ, short-form tasks.

llama_31_70b.notes: Meta flagship mid-size. Requires 2+ H100s at BF16. Strong reasoning, coding, and instruction following.

llama_31_405b.notes: Meta frontier open-weight. Requires 8-GPU node (tensor parallelism). Use only when closed-frontier quality needed at scale.

qwen3_32b.comparable_api: Competitive with Claude Sonnet on coding and reasoning
qwen3_32b.notes: Alibaba open-weight. Strong coding/math. Single H100 fits at BF16. Good cost-quality balance.

coreweave_h100.notes: Purpose-built GPU cloud. No hyperscaler overhead. Strong for LLM inference. $49.24/hr per 8-GPU HGX node normalized to per-GPU.

lambda_labs_h100.notes: Researcher-friendly neocloud. $2.99-3.99/hr per GPU range; using $3.29 midpoint. Limited SLA guarantees.

aws_trainium2.notes: Capacity Block reservations only — no on-demand pricing. trn2.48xlarge (16 chips) effective rate ~$29.60/hr instance = $1.85/chip. Requires Neuron SDK. Used to train Claude. Verify at aws.amazon.com/ec2/capacityblocks/pricing/

gcp_tpu_v5.notes: $1.20/chip-hour on-demand in us-central1 (confirmed cloud.google.com/tpu/pricing 2026-06-28). ~35% cheaper than CoreWeave H100. Best for JAX/TensorFlow workloads.
